title: Aulomyrcia linearifolia
taxonomy: Aulomyrcia linearifolia belongs to the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and widespread family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes over 6,000 species and is one of the largest and most diverse plant families found in the world. The genus Aulomyrcia is a member of the tribe Myrteae, which is a group of closely related genera within the Myrtaceae family. The plant's classification places it within the clade Rosidae, which includes many other flowering plant families. Further classification of Aulomyrcia linearifolia places it within the clade Eurosidae, a subset of the larger Rosidae clade.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory infections.
